mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity cash flows, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show a positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate earnings growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a statistically significant positive Rank IC greater than 0.02 for 60-day forward returns over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which lifts equity valuations over the next month when inflation expectations remain anchored.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative 20-day forward returns when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4 percent, invalidating the supply-side premium.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently elevated consumer price indices combined with restrictive policy rates compress profit margins and increase borrowing costs, depressing S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL exceeds 310.0 while FEDFUNDS remains above 5.0 percent
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns average above zero when CPIAUCSL exceeds 310.0 and FEDFUNDS stays above 5.0 percent, contradicting the stagflation penalty.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending expectations and lifting S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE produces a negative Rank IC for 20-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales signals immediate consumer demand contraction, forcing earnings downgrades and triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week during high-rate environments.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month declines fail to predict negative 5-day SPX returns when FEDFUNDS is below 2.5 percent, breaking the regime dependency.
